subject: English
chapter_name: The Case of the Fifth Word
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the plot and characters of the mystery story
Analyse how clues help solve a mystery
Interpret coded messages and hidden meanings
Develop logical reasoning and observation skills
Understand how detectives analyse information step by step
Strengthen reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Mystery and Detective Stories
topics_covered: Role of clues and reasoning in detective stories
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with examples of mystery stories
Reading the opening paragraph of the story
student_activities: List qualities of a good detective
Discuss how mysteries are solved in stories

lesson_plan.title: Characters and Story Background
topics_covered: Chief Brown’s role as a police officer
Background of Tim Nolan and Daniel Davenport
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the robbery case
student_activities: Discuss why Nolan and Davenport were suspected

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Mystery
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why the note was suspicious
student_activities: Predict what the coded words might mean
Discuss possible hiding places for the jewellery

lesson_plan.title: Decoding the Message
topics_covered: Connection between the words and the days of the week
Concept of hidden clues in mystery stories
student_activities: Decode the four words in groups

lesson_plan.title: Logical Reasoning and Story Climax
topics_covered: How Encyclopedia Brown solved the mystery
Discovery of the jewellery under the fir tree
student_activities: Explain the reasoning used by Encyclopedia
Write a short summary of how the mystery was solved

lesson_plan.title: Language Practice and Revision
student_activities: Convert direct speech into indirect speech

teacher_tip: Before revealing the answer, encourage students to analyse the coded words themselves. This helps them practise logical thinking and understand how detectives interpret clues.
